original_abstract: Detecting small objects in remote sensing imagery remains a significant challenge due to heavy background noise and the scarcity of pixel-level information, often leading to suboptimal performance by standard detection frameworks. To address these limitations, we propose LAR-YOLOv8, an enhanced detection algorithm derived from the YOLOv8 architecture. Our approach introduces three key innovations: First, we refine the feature extraction network by integrating a dual-branch attention mechanism to bolster local feature capture alongside a vision transformer block to maximize global feature representation. Second, we design an attention-guided bidirectional feature pyramid network (ABFPN) that utilizes a dynamic sparse attention mechanism. This structure efficiently extracts details from shallow layers and incorporates additional top-down paths to guide feature fusion across the network. Finally, we introduce the RIOU loss function to prevent gradient loss and ensure superior shape consistency between predicted and ground-truth bounding boxes. Comprehensive evaluations on the NWPU VHR-10, RSOD, and CARPK datasets demonstrate that LAR-YOLOv8 delivers substantial improvements in mAP and small-object detection accuracy while maintaining competitive model parameters and inference speeds. These results validate the effectiveness of our modifications for robust remote sensing applications.
humanized_abstract: Object detection in remote sensing imagery is still a difficult task, owing to large amounts of background noise and limited pixel information in the images, which severely limits the performance of detectors. To improve the performance of object detection in remote sensing images, we propose a novel detection network called LAR-YOLOv8 based on YOLOv8. To this end, the improvements made to the network include the following three points: 1) In the feature network part, the enhanced network is obtained by combining the dual-branch attention mechanism to enhance the local features, the vision transformer block (ViTB16) to strengthen the global features, so as to effectively learn and obtain more rich and strong features. 2) In the structure part, an attention-guided bidirectional feature pyramid network (ABFPN) is proposed by utilizing the dynamic sparse attention mechanism in shallow layers and adding more top-down paths to guide the feature fusion. 3) In the loss part, a novel loss function named RIOU loss is proposed to prevent gradient loss and strengthen the model's shape awareness for the predictive bounding box and the ground-truth bounding box. Experimental results conducted on NWPU VHR-10, RSOD, and CARPK datasets demonstrate that LAR-YOLOv8 achieves remarkable improvement in terms of mAP and small object detection performance while maintaining reasonable parameters and speed.

document.output: Object detection in remote sensing imagery is still a difficult task, owing to large amounts of background noise and limited pixel information in the images, which severely limits the performance of detectors. To improve the performance of object detection in remote sensing images, we propose a novel detection network called LAR-YOLOv8 based on YOLOv8. To this end, the improvements made to the network include the following three points: 1) In the feature network part, the enhanced network is obtained by combining the dual-branch attention mechanism to enhance the local features, the vision transformer block (ViTB16) to strengthen the global features, so as to effectively learn and obtain more rich and strong features. 2) In the structure part, an attention-guided bidirectional feature pyramid network (ABFPN) is proposed by utilizing the dynamic sparse attention mechanism in shallow layers and adding more top-down paths to guide the feature fusion. 3) In the loss part, a novel loss function named RIOU loss is proposed to prevent gradient loss and strengthen the model's shape awareness for the predictive bounding box and the ground-truth bounding box. Experimental results conducted on NWPU VHR-10, RSOD, and CARPK datasets demonstrate that LAR-YOLOv8 achieves remarkable improvement in terms of mAP and small object detection performance while maintaining reasonable parameters and speed.
